app.title: Upriv
vault.list.title: Active vaults
vault.list.subtitle: Securely managing your encrypted directories and cold storage.
vault.list.sort.title: Sort vaults
vault.list.sort.mode.name: Name
vault.list.view.title: Vault layout
vault.name.label: Vault name
vault.name.invalid_chars: Name cannot contain \ / : * ? " < > | or control characters.
vault.name.trailing: Name cannot end with a space or period.
vault.name.reserved: This name is reserved by the operating system.
vault.name.duplicate: An item with this name already exists here.
vault.order.label: Display order
vault.order.help: Lower numbers appear first in the vault list.

vault.import.title: Import archive

vault.create.title: New vault
vault.create.source_intro: Import an existing .7z archive or create a new empty vault.
vault.create.option.import_desc: Use a password-protected archive you already have. Upriv copies it into the vault folder.
vault.create.option.scratch_desc: Start with an empty vault and a new password.
vault.create.identity_intro: Display name and optional note. You can change these later in vault settings.
vault.create.password_import_intro: Enter the archive password and test it before continuing. Optional hint is stored in config only.
vault.create.password_scratch_intro: Choose a new vault password. Optional hint is a reminder — never the password itself.
vault.create.general_intro: Common options for idle auto-close, quit behavior, backups, and archive mode.
vault.create.advanced_intro: Optional expert settings. Defaults are safe for most users.
vault.create.settings_editable_later: All settings can be changed later in vault settings.
vault.create.error.duplicate: A vault with this name already exists.
vault.create.error.source_missing: Choose how to create the vault.
vault.create.error.password_not_validate: Test the archive password before creating.
vault.create.error.generic: Fix the highlighted step before creating.
vault.create.password_hint_help: A short reminder for you — do not repeat the password here.
vault.create.note_help: Short annotation for this vault (max {max} characters).
vault.change_password.title: Change password
vault.change_password.success: Password changed. Main archive and store use the new password.
vault.change_password.same_as_current: New password must be different from the current one.
close.dialog.title: Close vault
close.dialog.close_hint: Keeps encrypted cache on disk for faster reopen.
close.dialog.seal_confirm: Only the .7z file will remain. Encrypted cache on disk will be erased.
modal.file_manager.title: Files — {name}
modal.file_manager.placeholder_body: Browsing and editing for “{name}” will appear here in a later step. This window is scoped to this vault only.
modal.file_manager.dock.label: Minimized file managers
modal.file_manager.explorer.title: Explorer
modal.file_manager.tabs.label: Open files
modal.file_manager.viewer.empty_body: Choose a file in the tree to open it in a tab.
modal.file_manager.viewer.preview_unavai: “{name}” cannot be previewed in the browser yet. External editors will use the vault mount later.
modal.file_manager.delete.title: Delete permanently
modal.file_manager.delete.body_file: “{name}” will be permanently deleted. This cannot be undone.
modal.file_manager.delete.body_folder: “{name}” and everything inside it will be permanently deleted. This cannot be undone.
modal.file_manager.unsaved.title: Unsaved changes
modal.file_manager.unsaved.body: This file has unsaved changes. Discard them and continue?
modal.file_manager.toast.open_system: Would open in the system file manager (mock).
modal.file_manager.toast.open_terminal: Would open in terminal at this path (mock).
modal.file_manager.toast.copy_failed: Could not copy path to clipboard.
modal.backup.title: Backups — {name}
modal.backup.hint: Each backup keeps the password from when it was created. Opening old backups requires that password.
modal.backup.empty: No backups for this vault yet.
modal.backup.delete_confirm_one: Type the vault name to confirm:
modal.logs.title: Logs
modal.logs.hint: Plain-text diagnostic files from `.upriv/logs`. The active file is still being written.
modal.logs.delete_confirm_one: Delete this log file? This cannot be undone.
modal.logs.delete_confirm_many: Delete {count} log files? This cannot be undone.
modal.settings.title: Vault settings — {name}
modal.settings.section.storage_intro: How your files live on disk while the vault is open.
modal.settings.section.seven_zip_intro: On close, your vault is saved as a password-protected .7z file — the same format as 7-Zip, so you can open it elsewhere with the password.
modal.settings.save_confirm: Save changes to this vault? Some options apply on the next close or unlock.
modal.settings.change_password_help: Works with the vault open or closed. You must enter the current password; the app verifies it, re-encrypts the archive and store in RAM, then replaces the old files. Existing backups keep their original password.
modal.settings.field.vault.hidden: Hide this vault in the list
modal.settings.field.vault.hidden_help: Hidden vaults stay on disk but are omitted from the list until you show hidden vaults in system settings.
modal.settings.field.vault.order_help: Lower numbers appear higher in the vault list.
modal.settings.field.security.password_h: Optional. Shown when unlocking this vault. A short reminder only — never put the password here.
modal.settings.field.storage.mode_help: Choose how the vault works between open and close.
modal.settings.field.close.default_actio: Lock closes the vault from the list. Choose what stays on disk afterward.
modal.settings.field.close.secure_wipe_w: Without secure wipe, only unlinked files are removed — plaintext may remain recoverable.
modal.settings.field.security.mode_help: Controls only the vault unlock password. Session RAM keeps it in memory until you close the app or lock the vault; the other option asks again when you open and when you close, without keeping it in RAM.
modal.settings.field.auto_close.close_on: Close vault when quitting the app
Independent of idle auto-close. When enabled, open vaults are closed during app shutdown.
modal.settings.field.close.require_unmou: Independent of idle auto-close. When enabled, the virtual mount is torn down if the OS suspends or hibernates.
modal.settings.field.seven_zip.archive_m: How the .7z is built on the next close.
modal.settings.field.seven_zip.encrypt_f: Recommended. Names are hidden inside the .7z until you enter the password.
If disabled, anyone can list file and folder names in the .7z without the password — only the file contents stay encrypted.
modal.settings.field.policy.external_edi: While this vault is open, files appear on a virtual mount. Choose whether only Upriv or also system apps may open them.
modal.settings.option.policy.external_ed: VS Code, your file manager, and other system apps cannot access the mount path.
Editors and file managers can open files through the mount.
modal.settings.field.policy.copy_outside: Whether drag-and-drop, Save As, or shell copy to Desktop, USB, or /tmp is blocked at the mount layer (independent of the setting above).
modal.settings.option.policy.copy_block_: Try to prevent copies to Desktop, removable drives, and temp folders while the vault is open.
modal.settings.option.policy.copy_allow_: Processes that can access the mount may leave plaintext outside the vault folder.
modal.settings.option.security.session_r: + Unlock once per session; faster lock and reopen. − Password stays in RAM until you quit the app or lock the vault.
modal.settings.option.security.prompt_op: + Password never kept in RAM. − You must enter it every time you open and close the vault.
modal.settings.option.security.disk_clos: + Lock without retyping after the first unlock. − Stores a session file on disk; opening still asks for the password.
modal.settings.option.security.disk_open: Disk session for open and close
+ Open and close without retyping. − Session file on disk — less secure than asking each time.
modal.settings.option.storage.encrypted_: + Files stay encrypted on disk; you edit via a virtual folder. − Uses more RAM; recommended.
modal.settings.option.storage.plain_desc: + Normal folder on disk; simpler, less RAM. − Decrypted files on disk while open; forensic tools may recover traces after close (especially on SSD). Use only if needed.
modal.settings.option.close.close_desc: + Faster reopen; encrypted cache stays on disk. − Uses more space; local data remains until you seal.
modal.settings.option.close.seal_desc: + Only the portable archive; less on disk. − Slower reopen; rebuilds from the .7z each time.
modal.settings.option.seven_zip.encrypt_: + Faster close and reopen. − Larger .7z (no compression).
modal.settings.option.seven_zip.compress: + Smaller .7z on disk. − Slower; compresses then encrypts.
modal.note.title: Note — {name}
modal.note.placeholder: Add a short note for this vault…
modal.settings.danger_zone_help: Permanently removes this vault’s archive, store, config, and backups.
modal.settings.delete_confirm: Type the vault name to confirm:
modal.app_settings.title: System settings
modal.app_settings.save_confirm: Save system settings? Language and theme apply immediately.
modal.app_settings.section.appearance_in: Language and theme for the whole app.
modal.app_settings.field.hidden_vaults_h: Hidden vaults are marked per vault in vault settings. Choose when they appear in the list.
modal.app_settings.field.show_hidden_vau: Does not save — hidden vaults stay visible until you quit the app.
modal.app_settings.field.always_show_hid: Saved in settings. Hidden vaults appear in the list every time you open the app.
modal.app_settings.section.logging_intro: Diagnostic logs under `.upriv/logs` in your Upriv root folder.
modal.app_settings.section.behavior_intr: Where Upriv looks for your data. This is the folder that contains `.upriv/` — not an individual vault.
modal.app_settings.section.hidden_vaults: Hidden vaults are marked per vault in vault settings. Choose when they appear in the list.
modal.app_settings.field.locale_help: UI strings only; vault content is unchanged.
modal.app_settings.field.theme_help: Dark is the default. Theme applies when you save.
modal.app_settings.field.file_manager_do: When file managers are minimized, show all chips or only a single button to expand them. Saved in settings.
modal.app_settings.field.logging_enabled: Write app events to log files on disk.
modal.app_settings.field.logging_level_h: Minimum severity to write. Each level includes everything more severe below it (Error ⊂ Warn ⊂ Info ⊂ Debug).
modal.app_settings.field.upriv_root_mode: Choose one way to locate your data. Only one mode applies at a time.
modal.app_settings.option.upriv_root.aut: Search near the app for a folder with `.upriv/settings.toml`. Recommended for portable installs on a drive.
modal.app_settings.option.upriv_root.fix: Always open this Upriv root, regardless of where the app is launched from.
modal.app_settings.field.upriv_root_help: The folder that contains `.upriv/settings.toml`, `vaults/`, and `workspace/`. Each vault lives inside `vaults/<id>/`.
Warnings and errors; skips routine info.
Info, warnings, and errors — normal use. Recommended.
Everything including debug detail; more disk use.
modal.help.title: Help
modal.help.hint: Short guides for Upriv. Search filters sections in the current language.
modal.help.body.overview.1: Upriv manages encrypted vaults. Each vault is saved as a password-protected .7z archive — the same format as 7-Zip, so you can open it elsewhere with the password.
modal.help.body.overview.2: A vault can be open (you edit files), closed (encrypted cache stays on disk for faster reopen), or sealed (only the portable .7z remains). The list shows the current state with a colored badge.
modal.help.body.overview.3: By default, files stay encrypted on disk while the vault is open. You work through a virtual folder; plaintext is not written to your hard drive in production mode.
modal.help.body.vault_list.1: The home screen lists every vault in your Upriv root folder. Use the sort menu to order by position, name, state, or last accessed.
modal.help.body.vault_list.2: The view menu changes layout density: default rows, large cards, compact rows, or block tiles. Sort and view preferences are remembered in system settings.
modal.help.body.vault_list.3: When sorted by position, drag the handle on a row to reorder vaults. Refresh rescans the vault folder on disk (mock in this demo).
modal.help.body.open_close.1: Unlock asks for the vault password. Lock runs the close pipeline: test the existing archive, build a new .7z from session content, verify it, then swap files atomically.
modal.help.body.open_close.2: Close keeps an encrypted cache on disk so the next open is faster. Seal removes that cache and leaves only the .7z archive — best for portability, slower to reopen.
modal.help.body.open_close.3: When locking, you can pick close or seal if the vault default allows it. Idle auto-close locks after inactivity (optional). You can also close open vaults when quitting the app — independent of the idle timer.
modal.help.body.vault_settings.1: Open settings from a vault row to change display name, list order, note, storage mode, default close action, backups, password hint, password-in-memory behavior, 7-Zip options, and access policy while open.
modal.help.body.vault_settings.2: Save writes config.toml on disk. Some options apply on the next unlock or close. Change password re-encrypts the main archive and store; existing backups keep their original password.
modal.help.body.backups.1: When enabled, Upriv can create a backup of the .7z on each close. Each backup keeps the password from when it was created.
modal.help.body.backups.2: Open Backups from a vault row to list, restore, download, or delete backup files. Deleting requires typing the vault name to confirm.
modal.help.body.system_settings.1: System settings (⋯ menu) control language, theme, logging, and where Upriv looks for data. Language changes UI strings only — vault content is unchanged.
modal.help.body.system_settings.2: The Upriv root folder contains `.upriv/settings.toml`, `vaults/`, `workspace/`, and `logs/`. It is not an individual vault. Choose auto-detect (portable) or a fixed folder — not both.
modal.help.body.logs.1: View logs (⋯ menu) lists plain-text diagnostic files under `.upriv/logs`. The active file is still being written and cannot be deleted.
modal.help.body.logs.2: Open a file to read parsed lines, or select entries to download a zip or delete old logs. Log level in system settings controls how much is written.
modal.help.body.security.1: Vault passwords live in RAM during a session in v1. They are never stored in settings files, browser storage, or log files.
modal.help.body.security.2: Encrypted directory mode is recommended: files stay encrypted on disk and you edit via a virtual mount. Plain workspace mode writes decrypted files to disk while open — simpler but less secure.
modal.help.body.security.3: Access policy controls whether system apps can open the mount and whether copy-out to Desktop, USB, or temp folders is blocked. Blocking copy completely while allowing external editors is hard to enforce.
modal.help.body.recovery.1: If the .7z archive and encrypted store no longer match, the vault enters recovery instead of silently opening as closed.
modal.help.body.recovery.2: Recovery offers: use the encrypted store, re-import the .7z, compare both sides, or discard the workspace. Pick the option that matches what you trust on disk.
unlock.title: Unlock vault
recovery.title: Recovery required
warning.vault_open_on_exit: A vault is still open. Close it before exiting?
warning.external_editor: External apps may copy files outside the vault (temp folders).
warning.policy.external_block_copy: Blocking copy while allowing external apps is hard to enforce completely. Editors often create temp files outside the vault.
warning.plain_mode: Plain mode stores decrypted files on disk until close. Not recommended.
warning.password_change_backups: Existing backups will keep the old password. Only the main archive and new backups will use the new password.
warning.auto_close_soon: Vault will close in {seconds} s due to inactivity.
error.sync_mismatch: Archive and encrypted store are out of sync
empty.vaults_all_hidden: All vaults are hidden — show hidden vaults in system settings
empty.workspace_closed: Unlock a vault to access files
